_comment: ISO 8583 example — an authorization request (MTI 0100) expressed as a JSON field map. The wire format of ISO 8583 is binary (a Message Type Indicator + one or two bitmaps that flag which numbered data elements are present), so it is not human-readable on the wire. This JSON is the documented field-definition form most catalogs/parsers use: `mti` plus the numbered Data Elements (DE). Each DE has a fixed meaning, length, and type — they become the typed properties of the message class.
name: Authorization Request
